scenarios.title: Edit the approved memo after the fact
scenarios.simple: Someone doctors the memo bytes in the audit bundle and hopes nobody notices.
scenarios.how: The disclosed bytes no longer match the artifact's content address. One hash comparison catches it.

scenarios.title: Forge a record with a lookalike identity
scenarios.simple: An impostor re-signs altered content under a self-issued certificate that copies the real CA's name.
scenarios.how: Caught twice: the certificate does not chain to the trusted CA, and the altered record fails its log inclusion proof.

scenarios.title: Hide the failed AI attempt
scenarios.simple: Delete the embarrassing first try from the history handed to the auditor.
scenarios.how: The successful retry's prev_attempt link points at the missing record. The hole is structural and visible.

scenarios.title: Rewrite the entire history log
scenarios.simple: An insider rebuilds the whole log with one record swapped, re-signing everything with the real log key.
scenarios.how: The rewritten history cannot produce a consistency proof back to the externally anchored root. The past is out of reach.
